body:
Add authentication to the wfd-os portal to control access and prevent unauthorized LLM usage costs.

## Context
The portal is deployed publicly at computingforall.watechcoalition.org. Pages that call LLM-powered backends (chat, consulting, marketing) cannot be enabled until auth gates access. Without auth, anyone with the URL can generate unbounded LLM costs.

## Recommended: Magic Link Authentication (passwordless)

Easiest to implement in Python. No passwords to manage, no OAuth complexity.

### Option A: starlette-admin + itsdangerous (simplest, no external service)
- User enters email → server generates a signed token (itsdangerous) → sends email via Graph API (already configured) → user clicks link → token verified → session cookie set
- Pros: zero external dependencies, works with existing Graph email setup, 50 lines of code
- Cons: roll-your-own session management, no SSO

### Option B: authlib + magic link flow
- Same magic link UX but uses authlib for JWT session tokens
- Pros: standards-based JWT, refresh tokens, better session management
- Cons: slightly more setup than itsdangerous

### Option C: Azure AD B2C (already provisioned)
- 3 B2C directories already exist (computingforallusers, computingforallstage, computingforall003)
- Supports magic link via "Email OTP" policy
- Pros: enterprise SSO, MFA, managed service, role claims in token
- Cons: most complex setup, XML policy configuration, harder to debug

### Option D: Stytch or Descope (managed magic link SaaS)
- Third-party managed auth with Python SDK
- Pros: 5-minute integration, hosted UI, analytics
- Cons: external dependency, free tier limits, data leaves CFA infrastructure

## Recommendation
Start with Option A (itsdangerous signed tokens + Graph email) — it's the fastest path using infrastructure we already have:
1. User visits /login, enters email
2. FastAPI gateway generates signed token with email + role + expiry (itsdangerous.URLSafeTimedSerializer)
3. Token sent via Microsoft Graph sendMail (EMAIL_SENDER=ritu@computingforall.org — already configured)
4. User clicks link → token verified → session cookie set with role (student/staff/admin)
5. Middleware checks session cookie on every /api/* request
6. Role determines tier: student = Tier 1 (read-only), staff = Tier 1 + Tier 2 (LLM)

Upgrade to Azure AD B2C (Option C) later if SSO or MFA is needed.

## Roles
- student: read-only data access (Tier 1 endpoints)
- staff: read + LLM features (Tier 1 + Tier 2 endpoints)
- admin: all access + user management

## Scope
- /login and /verify routes in the FastAPI gateway
- Session middleware on all /api/* routes
- Rate limiting: 100 req/hour for students, 500 req/hour for staff
- Allowlist of authorized emails (start with CFA staff + enrolled students)

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: #11 (API gateway consolidation) — auth middleware goes on the gateway
- Blocks: #12 (deploy read-only DB), #9 (credential rotation for LLM keys)

## Python packages
- itsdangerous (token signing — already in most Flask/FastAPI stacks)
- python-multipart (form handling for login page)
- Or: authlib (if JWT preferred over signed cookies)
title: feat: authentication — magic link + role-based access for wfd-os portal
